name: Givelight Foundation
mission: Givelight Foundations primary exempt purpose is to provide shelter, clothing and food to children who have been orphaned by a natural disaster or other global crisis; to provide educational, spiritual and social services to the children residing in these orphanages.
theoryOfChange: By providing high-quality education and a loving family environment (feeding the body, mind, and soul), orphans can reach their highest human potential and break the cycle of poverty.
scoreSummary: GiveLight Foundation shows strong alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: P&G Humanitarian Award (to founder Dian Alyan) (P&G Alumni)
Gratitude Network Fellow (to founder Dian Alyan) (Gratitude Network)

zakatClaimEvidence: Zakat: Used strictly for essentials like food, clothing, books, and healthcare. More info. ... Zakat can be paid to the following who fall into one or more of eight zakatable categories designated by God in the Quran (9:60). ... Collectors of Zakat (i.e. GiveLight Foundation). (Source: https://givelight.org/donate/)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: 59% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 135 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 5.9% of revenue ($134,108 on $2,270,848)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue <$5M, CEO pay above 5% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

components.evidence: Accepts zakat (+2); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Humanitarian service (+2)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 38/50: HIGH Muslim donor fit, education global cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: Separate restricted/endowment funds from operating reserves and publish a clear reserve-use policy.
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Document a clear theory of change with an explicit causal pathway.
components.name: Theory of Change

all_citations.claim: GiveLight Foundation holds a Charity Navigator score of 94.66666666666667/100.
The organization is zakat-eligible and provides specific policies for zakat donations.
The organization reported total revenue of $2,270,848 and a program expense ratio of 88.7%.
GiveLight operates the Rahmatan lil ‘Alamain home in Indonesia.
The foundation tracks outcomes such as university degrees attained and meals provided per day.
Donors have multiple giving methods to support orphan care and educational programs.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a solid overall rating due to its high financial transparency and efficient use of capital, though its total score is moderated by medium data confidence levels.
areas_for_improvement: Improving data confidence and third-party impact verification to move beyond medium-level reporting.
Expanding public documentation of long-term beneficiary outcomes beyond the 2019 reporting cycle.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The charity shows high alignment for Muslim donors through its zakat-eligible status and focus on global education and orphan care [2][6]. Its track record includes establishing dedicated homes like the Noordeen in Sri Lanka and the Rahmatan lil ‘Alamain in Indonesia [4][5].
dimension_explanations.impact: GiveLight demonstrates direct service effectiveness by maintaining a high program expense ratio of 88.7% and achieving a Charity Navigator score of 94.66666666666667/100 [1][3]. The foundation has successfully scaled its model to provide university degrees and daily meals for orphans across multiple international sites [5].

baseline_narrative.headline: GiveLight Foundation provides a global network of loving homes and educational support for orphaned children facing extreme circumstances.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 88.7%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach beneficiaries.
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of $0.00 per $1 raised.
Strong financial stability with 135.0 months of working capital.
baseline_narrative.summary: GiveLight Foundation is a crisis relief organization that builds sustainable homes and families for orphans in countries including Indonesia, Sri Lanka, and Bangladesh [4][5]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill their religious obligations through direct support of vulnerable children [2][6].

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a solid overall rating due to its high financial transparency and efficient use of capital, though its total score is moderated by medium data confidence levels.

areas_for_improvement.context: The organization currently operates with a <cite id="15">board size of 4 members</cite>, which is considered minimal for an organization of this asset size.
While internal outcomes are tracked, there is a need to <cite id="16">document a clear theory of change</cite> with explicit causal pathways to improve impact scores.

bbb_assessment.summary: Financial statements are audited and show exceptional efficiency, though full BBB accreditation is not on file.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization's massive <cite id="9">170-month reserve</cite> mitigates any immediate risk from fluctuating annual revenue.
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve <cite id="15">limited board oversight</cite> and a <cite id="22">-3.3% three-year revenue CAGR</cite>, indicating slightly declining year-over-year funding.

confidence.notes: Strong financial efficiency data, but governance and growth metrics show room for improvement.

alignment.explanation: Strong fit for Muslim donors as the charity <cite id="7">accepts zakat</cite> and serves <cite id="20">eight categories of asnaf</cite>, including the poor and wayfarers.
alignment.improvement: The charity could further improve alignment by <cite id="21">expanding services to underserved geographies</cite> with limited existing nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust GiveLight due to its <cite id="13">perfect 100 Leadership score</cite> and <cite id="12">94.67 overall rating</cite> from Charity Navigator. The founder, Dian Alyan, has received the <cite id="17">P&G Humanitarian Award</cite> for her work.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity should expand its board and <cite id="18">publish a clear reserve-use policy</cite> for its significant assets.

impact.explanation: Operations are highly efficient with a <cite id="4">88.7% program expense ratio</cite>, though the <cite id="19">cost per beneficiary of $1,377.81</cite> is standard for high-quality residential care.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="16">formalizing a theory of change</cite> to better map how educational interventions lead to long-term poverty alleviation.

rich_narrative.headline: GiveLight Foundation provides a global network of loving homes and educational support for orphaned children facing extreme circumstances.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize maximum financial efficiency and want their zakat to fund permanent, high-quality residential care and education for orphans.
donor_motivations: Want 88.7% of their donation to reach the field
Value long-term educational outcomes over short-term relief
Seek to fulfill zakat through an organization with strong Islamic values
Prefer supporting established organizations with significant financial reserves
giving_considerations: The organization has very high reserves, meaning your gift supports long-term sustainability rather than urgent survival
Small board size may mean less diverse institutional oversight
Focus is on holistic care, which has a higher cost-per-beneficiary than simple food distribution
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fund rapid-response emergency aid or those who prefer organizations with large, independent governing boards.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified program delivery and some outcome tracking, but lacks independent RCT-level evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: GiveLight provides stable housing and education to break the cycle of poverty for orphans, though this pathway is not yet formally documented in a TOC chart.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the high cost of residential care translates into permanent self-sufficiency for the children.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While they have high reserves, their <cite id="22">revenue has slightly declined</cite>, and they have <cite id="24">plans to build more homes globally</cite>.

strengths.detail: The organization operates with a <cite id="4">88.7% program expense ratio</cite> and spends <cite id="5">$0.00 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions.
GiveLight holds an <cite id="8">overall score of 94.94.66666666666667/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting strong governance and transparency.
The charity maintains <cite id="9">135.0 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, ensuring long-term sustainability for its orphanages.

strengths_deep_dive: GiveLight's operational model is distinguished by its long-term commitment to beneficiaries, moving beyond emergency aid to permanent infrastructure. They currently manage <cite id="10">orphanage construction and operation</cite> alongside specialized programs like the <cite id="11">Family Scholarships Program</cite>. Their financial health is verified by a <cite id="12">94.67 overall rating from Charity Navigator</cite>, which includes a <cite id="13">perfect 100 score for Leadership</cite>. This stability is backed by <cite id="14">$11,280,674 in total assets</cite>, providing a massive safety net for the children in their care.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 2005, GiveLight Foundation is a dedicated humanitarian organization focused on <cite id="1">building loving homes and families for orphans</cite> in response to extreme global crises. The organization operates a sophisticated network of care across multiple countries, including <cite id="2">Indonesia, Sri Lanka, Bangladesh, and Pakistan</cite>, providing not just shelter but long-term educational pathways. In the 2023 fiscal year, GiveLight reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $2,186,834</cite>, maintaining a remarkably lean operation with a <cite id="4">88.7% program expense ratio</cite> according to recent financial filings. This financial efficiency is further highlighted by a <cite id="5">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, indicating that the organization relies heavily on organic support and volunteer-driven initiatives rather than paid solicitation. Beyond immediate relief, GiveLight invests in the future of its beneficiaries through a <cite id="6">Higher Education Fund and K-12 Scholarships</cite>, ensuring that orphaned children have the resources to achieve self-sufficiency. The organization is <cite id="7">fully zakat-eligible</cite>, serving multiple categories of asnaf including the poor (fuqara) and needy (masakin), making it a primary choice for Muslim donors seeking to fulfill their religious obligations through high-impact orphan care.
